Article.ArticleTitle: Thermal stability, aqueous solubility, and hydration properties of L-citrulline from density, viscosity, and acoustic measurements, supported by DFT and MD simulations.

Abstract.AbstractText: The properties of L-citrulline and its aqueous solutions are investigated by thermogravimetry, solubility, density, viscosity, sound speed measurements, and computer simulations. Thermogravimetric analysis shows the onset of decomposition of L-citrulline at 486.15 K. A temperature-dependent increase in solubility from 102 to 210 g per 1000 g of water within 293.15-313.15 K was found. Thermodynamic analysis shows that the dissolution process is endothermic and driven by an increase in entropy. Apparent molar volumes and hydration numbers derived from compressibility and viscosity measurements emphasize the role of L-citrulline as a structure-forming agent. Computational methods, including density functional theory (DFT) and molecular dynamics simulations, confirm strong water interactions and provide insight into molecular stability and reactivity. Structural differences between L-citrulline and L-arginine show different hydration behavior and physicochemical properties, with L-citrulline exhibiting lower electrostrictive effects. These results improve understanding of L-citrulline hydration and provide insights for optimizing formulations in food, pharmaceutical, and sports nutrition supplements.
